title: Sonnet - To Zante
content:
Fair isle, that from the fairest of all flowers,
 Thy gentlest of all gentle names dost take!
 How many memories of what radiant hours
 At sight of thee and thine at once awake!
 How many scenes of what departed bliss!
 How many thoughts of what entombed hopes!
 How many visions of a maiden that is
 No more, no more upon thy verdant slopes!
 No more! alas, that magical sad sound
 Transforming all! Thy charms shall please no more,
 Thy memory no more! Accursed ground
 Henceforth I hold thy flower-enameled shore,
 O hyacinthine isle! O purple Zante!
 "Isola d'oro! Fior di Levante!"
meaning:
This evocative piece by Edgar Allan Poe, titled "Sonnet - To Zante", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
